---
title: "ST512-SP26-Homework-6"
author: "Jason Menjivar-Hernandez"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Problem 1 

**a**

$H_o: \sigma_{\alpha}^2$ = 0 

$H_A: \sigma_{\alpha}^2$ > 0 

F.value = $\frac{MSA}{MSE}$ = 238.7

p.value $\approx$ 0.0000 

Reject null hypothesis. There is strong evidence that mean sodium content is NOT the same in all brands. 

**b**

$\bar{y}_{1.} \approx$ 23.64 mg 

$\bar{y}_{2.} \approx$ 10.68 mg 

**c**

$\sigma^2$ = MSE = 0.72 

$\hat{\sigma}_\mu$ = $S^2_\mu$ = $\frac{MS_{Tr}-MSE}{n}$ = $\frac{170.91-0.72}{8}$ = 21.274 

**d**

$\hat{p}$ = $\frac{21.274}{0.72+21.274}$ = 0.967 

Therefore, approximately 97% of variation in the sodium content is due to the different brands. 

## Problem 2 

**a**
